chunks.text: SM/PS ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. No. D-8061 of 2017 Ms. Rahila Anjum Ansari & others Versus Federation of Pakistan & others ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- BEFORE: Justice Irfan Saadat Khan, Justice Muhammad Faisal Kamal Alam,JJ 1. For hearing of CMA No.12158/2021 2. For hearing of CMA No.1493/2020 3. For hearing of CMA No.21267/2018 4. For hearing of CMA No.33685/2017 (stay) 5. For hearing of main case. --------- 23.09.2021 Ms. Rukhsana Umar, advocate for the petitioners Mr. Khursheed Javed, Assistant Attorney General. Mr. Muhammad Abdullah, advocate for K-electric. -.----.---.- The instant petition has been filed on the ground that there was some dispute between the petitioner and the K-electric authorities about payment of the bills. Thereafter it was agreed between the parties that whatever are the outstanding bills, which the petitioner have to pay, will be recovered by the K-electric authorities in 22 equal monthly installments. The counsel for the petitioner states that the petitioner is ready to pay the outstanding amount but not the amount, which has already been declared null and void by the NEPRA through their order dated 18.7.2017. Counsel appearing for K-electric authorities stated that the petitioner is only entitled for the benefit of installment till the time no default in respect of payment of the outstanding dues alongwith payment of current bills is being made by the petitioner. We after hearing the instant matter at some length dispose of the same with the consent of the parties in the following manner;- i.

Counsel appearing for K-electric authorities stated that the petitioner is only entitled for the benefit of installment till the time no default in respect of payment of the outstanding dues alongwith payment of current bills is being made by the petitioner. We after hearing the instant matter at some length dispose of the same with the consent of the parties in the following manner;- i. That the K-electric authorities would not demand any amount from the petitioner, being arrears of K-electric bills, which has 2 already been declared to be null and void by the NEPRA vide order dated 18.7.2017. ii. That admitted outstanding amounts against the petitioner would be recovered in 22 equal monthly installments and the petitioner is under obligation to pay the current bills as well as the installment of the arrears without any fail. It is clarified that if the petitioner fails to pay the current bills or the installments of the arrears bills, the facility granted to them could be recalled / withdrawn by the K-electric authorities without any further notice. With these directions the instant petition alongwith all the listed and pending applications stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE SM
